exclusion_rules.description: Only bona fide residents of Mizoram are eligible. The scheme is universal across all residents regardless of income — general public, government employees, pensioners, and AB PM-JAY beneficiaries.

General-public and government-employee/pensioner beneficiaries must be enrolled under a contribution plan to be active. AB PM-JAY beneficiaries pay no premium and are auto-covered. An unenrolled, non-PMJAY resident is not yet active until they enrol.

family_criteria.family_definition: All members of a bona fide Mizoram resident family. Coverage is a family floater of Rs 5 lakh per family per year for the general population. Eligible Category A civil pensioners (pension of Rs 20,000 or more, contributing Rs 1,000/month) get an enhanced Rs 12 lakh per family per year.

covered_procedures: Cashless hospitalization at state-run hospitals, primary health centres, and empanelled private or church-run hospitals
Orthopedics (joint replacement, fracture and spine surgery)
Selected OPD services not requiring pre-authorization
Medically referred treatment outside Mizoram under notified conditions
Enhanced Rs 12 lakh annual cover for eligible Category A civil pensioners
excluded_procedures: OPD services requiring pre-authorization (outside the notified covered set)
Treatment costs above the applicable annual family ceiling

required_documents.name: Aadhaar Card
required_documents.notes: Identity and e-KYC proof for all family members.

required_documents.name: Ration Card
required_documents.notes: Proof of Mizoram residence and family composition.

required_documents.name: Government Service / Pension ID
required_documents.notes: Required for government employees and pensioners to determine the applicable contribution plan and (for Category A pensioners) the enhanced Rs 12 lakh cover.

required_documents.name: Passport-size Photograph
required_documents.notes: For the beneficiary record and card.

required_documents.name: Bank Account Details
required_documents.notes: For premium contribution and reimbursement processing.

enrollment_channels: Mizoram State Health Care Society offices
District and state hospitals with MUHCS desk
enrollment_steps: Step 1: Identify your category — general public, government employee, civil pensioner, or AB PM-JAY beneficiary (PM-JAY beneficiaries pay no premium)

Step 2: Choose a contribution plan if applicable — General Plan Rs 2,500/year (general ward), Standard Plan Rs 5,000/year (semi-private ward), or Private Plan Rs 10,000/year (private ward); all give Rs 5 lakh cover
Step 3: Apply on the MUHCS portal (muhcs.mizoram.gov.in) or at a designated centre with Aadhaar, ration card, photo, and (if applicable) service/pension ID

Step 4: Pay the applicable premium (government employees contribute Rs 200-Rs 1,500/month by pay level; Category A pensioners Rs 1,000/month for Rs 12 lakh cover, Category B Rs 500/month for Rs 5 lakh)
Step 5: Receive the MUHCS beneficiary card/ID
Step 6: Present the card at a state-run, PHC, or empanelled private/church-run hospital for cashless treatment up to the applicable annual cover
description_for_embedding: The Mizoram Universal Healthcare Scheme (MUHCS), launched on 19 March 2025 and effective from April 2025, is Mizoram's flagship universal state health insurance scheme. It replaced and upgraded the earlier Mizoram State Health Care Scheme (MSHCS, which gave Rs 2 lakh cover) and provides Rs 5 lakh per family per year of cashless hospitalization to ALL bona fide residents of Mizoram regardless of income — covering the general public, government employees, civil pensioners, and Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY beneficiaries under one umbrella. Eligible Category A civil pensioners (pension of Rs 20,000 or more, contributing Rs 1,000/month) get an enhanced Rs 12 lakh per family per year. The scheme covers pre-existing conditions from enrollment and treatment at state-run hospitals, primary health centres, and empanelled private or church-run hospitals, including medically referred care outside the state. It is contribution-based for the general population: a General Plan of Rs 2,500/year (general ward), a Standard Plan of Rs 5,000/year (semi-private ward), and a Private Plan of Rs 10,000/year (private ward), all giving Rs 5 lakh cover; AB PM-JAY beneficiaries pay no premium and are auto-covered. Government employees contribute Rs 200 to Rs 1,500 per month by pay level. Enrollment is via the MUHCS portal (muhcs.mizoram.gov.in) or designated centres with Aadhaar, ration card, photograph, and service/pension ID where applicable. The scheme is administered by the Mizoram State Health Care Society. Helpline 0389-2328223.
